data.title: China Power’s SPIC Hydropower Q1 Net Profit Jumps Despite Revenue Decline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) is now available.

China Power International Development reported that its 55.13%-owned subsidiary SPIC Hydropower posted an 18.55% year-on-year decline in first-quarter 2026 revenue to RMB2.47 billion, but net profit attributable to shareholders surged 168% to RMB393 million. Basic earnings per share tripled to RMB0.09, supported by higher underlying profitability, as net profit excluding extraordinary items rose 176.2%, while total assets and shareholder equity grew modestly, though the company cautioned that these figures reflect only the subsidiary and not the group’s overall financial position.

SPIC Hydropower’s comparative figures for early 2025 were restated under PRC GAAP rules for business combinations under common control following an asset restructuring that made it a subsidiary of China Power. The restructuring has allowed SPIC Hydropower to account for acquired operations as if owned in prior periods, clarifying performance trends for investors, but China Power advised shareholders and potential investors not to rely solely on the subsidiary’s results when assessing the group as a whole.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock, see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong–incorporated power producer with a growing focus on hydropower through its majority-owned subsidiary SPIC Hydropower Co., Ltd., which is listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange. The group operates within China’s electricity sector, expanding its renewable generation portfolio and consolidating assets under common control to strengthen its position in low‑carbon power.

Average Trading Volume: 26,151,024

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$40.95B

data.title: Asia Strategy Digit Technology Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Routine Mandates and Share Issue Authority
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Jicheng Holdings Limited ( (HK:1027) ) has issued an announcement.

Asia Strategy Digit Technology Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 1 June 2026 in Hong Kong to present audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and to seek shareholder approval on a series of routine corporate matters. The agenda focuses on corporate governance continuity, with resolutions to re-elect two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, authorise the board to set directors’ pay, and re-appoint Infinity CPA Limited as auditor with board-approved remuneration.

Shareholders will also vote on a general mandate empowering the board to issue, allot and deal in new shares up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding rights issues, option exercises, scrip dividends and conversions of existing instruments. If approved, this mandate would give the board added flexibility to raise capital or pursue strategic transactions within prescribed limits, potentially influencing future financing activities and the company’s capital structure.

More about China Jicheng Holdings Limited

Asia Strategy Digit Technology Holdings Limited, formerly known as China Jicheng Holdings Limited, is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands. It operates under stock code 1027 on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, subject to the Listing Rules and corporate governance requirements applicable to listed issuers.

Average Trading Volume: 42,691

The latest update is out from Sunshine Oilsands ( (HK:2012) ).

Sunshine Oilsands has again postponed the mailing of a shareholder circular related to a proposed placing of up to 114,280,000 new shares under a specific mandate, pushing the expected despatch date from April 30 to on or before May 29, 2026. The delay, attributed to additional time needed to prepare and incorporate required information, adds further uncertainty around the timing of the share issuance and its completion, prompting the company to urge shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its Hong Kong-listed shares.

More about Sunshine Oilsands

Sunshine Oilsands Ltd. is a Calgary-based public company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, focused on developing its substantial oil sands leases in Canada’s Athabasca region. The company holds interests in roughly one million acres of oil sands and petroleum and natural gas leases, and is currently concentrating on advancing its West Ells project, which targets initial production of 5,000 barrels per day.

The latest update is out from Xinyi Glass Holdings ( (HK:0868) ).

Xinyi Glass Holdings has called its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK$0.215 per share. The meeting will also consider the re-election of several executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor, and authorisations for the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration.

Shareholders will be asked to renew a mandate allowing the board to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, over the coming year, subject to regulatory limits. They will also vote on a general mandate enabling the board to issue new shares or deal in treasury shares, providing management with capital management flexibility that could influence future funding, share capital structure and shareholder returns.

More about Xinyi Glass Holdings

Xinyi Glass Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed glass manufacturer, primarily engaged in producing and selling float glass, automobile glass and architectural glass for construction and automotive markets. The group serves both domestic and international customers, positioning itself as a major supplier within the broader building materials and auto supply chains in Greater China and beyond.

Kingland Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1751) ) has issued an update.

AI Energy Engineering Holdings has expanded its operational base in Hong Kong through new leases secured by its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, Kingland (Sino) Company Limited. The company is strengthening its physical infrastructure to support its engineering operations and maintain its presence in key local industrial areas.

On 24 April 2025, Kingland (Sino) entered into a three-year tenancy agreement with King Well Construction for Premises A in Yuen Long, covering about 20,000 square feet at a monthly rent of HK$100,000. On 18 September 2025, it signed a second five-year tenancy with Easy Stand for Premises B at a monthly rent of HK$110,000, with both leases treated as asset acquisitions under HKFRS 16 and classified as discloseable transactions under Hong Kong listing rules, requiring announcement but not shareholder approval.

An announcement from Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1171) ) is now available.

Yankuang Energy Group has completed the issuance of its 2026 first tranche of sci-tech innovation bonds designated for M&A activities, raising RMB 2 billion in China’s interbank bond market. The medium-term notes, carrying a 1.84% interest rate and a 3+N year term, were fully subscribed and funds were received on 29 April 2026, reflecting the company’s continued access to low-cost domestic funding channels and its intent to support merger-and-acquisition-driven growth.

China Merchants Bank acted as bookrunner and lead underwriter, with Industrial Bank and Agricultural Bank of China as joint-lead underwriters, underscoring strong backing from major Chinese financial institutions. The successful placement of these sci-tech innovation bonds is likely to strengthen Yankuang Energy’s balance sheet flexibility for future transactions and signals confidence from institutional investors in its credit profile and expansion strategy.

An update from Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Ltd. ( (HK:3718) ) is now available.

Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Limited announced that its 85%-owned Hong Kong subsidiary, Shiny Glory Services Limited, has won a major tender from the Leisure and Cultural Services Department to provide cleaning and supporting services to leisure venues across several Hong Kong districts. The three-year contracts, expected to commence on 1 May 2026, carry an estimated total value of HK$964.54 million and annual revenue of about HK$321.51 million, although final figures may vary as formal contracts have not yet been signed.

Under the new mandate, Shiny Glory Services will deliver cleansing, pest control, waste and recyclable collection, and other support services to parks, playgrounds, and leisure venues in Islands, Tsuen Wan, Tuen Mun, Yuen Long, and Kwai Tsing. The successful bid reinforces the group’s position in Hong Kong’s environmental hygiene market and signals a meaningful expansion of its services portfolio and revenue base, while the company cautions investors that actual contract value and income could change with market conditions.

More about Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Ltd.

Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Limited is an environmental services provider focused on environmental hygiene, hazardous waste treatment, and waste electrical and electronic equipment treatment. Through subsidiaries such as Shiny Glory Services Limited in Hong Kong, the group targets public-sector contracts for large-scale cleaning, waste management, and related support services across urban districts and public facilities.

The latest announcement is out from Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited ( (HK:0412) ).

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited has announced the current composition of its board of directors, led by chairman Kang Jian and vice chairman Zhu Jianbiao, and including a mix of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. The company also detailed the membership and chairmanship of its five key board committees—Executive, Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Strategic Development—clarifying governance responsibilities and oversight roles for each director, which provides investors and stakeholders with greater transparency on corporate decision-making and control.

The updated committee structure shows independent non-executive directors chairing critical oversight bodies such as the Audit and Remuneration Committees, while top executives lead the Executive and Strategic Development Committees. This delineation of roles is likely aimed at reinforcing checks and balances, strengthening corporate governance standards and signaling to the market that the company is aligning its board practices with Hong Kong listing expectations and investor demands for independent oversight.

More about Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating under stock code 412. It is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical diversified governance structure for a Hong Kong-listed infrastructure and investment group.

data.title: Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Names Kang Jian as New Chairman in Leadership Reshuffle
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited ( (HK:0412) ) just unveiled an update.

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited announced a leadership reshuffle effective 30 April 2026, with executive director and board chairman Li Tianzhang stepping down from his roles, including chairmanships of the executive and nomination committees, citing work adjustment and without any reported disagreements with the board. The company has appointed seasoned financier and economist Kang Jian as executive director and chairman, transferring to him the leadership of the executive and nomination committees, a move that brings extensive banking, investment and corporate management experience from major Chinese financial institutions and affiliated Shandong Hi-Speed entities, which may support the group’s strategic development and governance continuity.

Mr. Kang’s background includes senior roles at Shandong High Speed Sichuan Industrial Development, Qilu Expressway, Weihai Bank and Shandong Hi-speed Company Limited, as well as long tenures at Ping An Bank, Industrial Bank and China Construction Bank’s Shandong Branch, underpinned by degrees in investment economic management and finance from Shandong University of Finance and Economics. The board expressed respect and gratitude to Mr. Li for his contributions, while the appointment of Mr. Kang signals a continuation of state-linked infrastructure and financial sector expertise at the helm, likely reassuring investors and stakeholders about management stability during the transition.

More about Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in infrastructure-related investment and holdings. The group is affiliated with the wider Shandong Hi-Speed network of companies involved in expressways, industrial development and related financial interests in mainland China, reflecting a focus on transport, industrial assets and associated financial services.
